title: Bathroom & Kitchen fan-control PREVIEW bundle variants
description: Source-of-truth promotion of the Bathroom and Kitchen PoE room-bundle fan-control variants from planning-only (ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-VARIANTS-001) to an explicit PREVIEW bundle plan (ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-VARIANTS-002). The plan is that WebFlash easy mode becomes a bundle picker in which these fan-control variants MAY appear as Advanced-install-only, acknowledgement-gated PREVIEW bundle products (with warnings); the stable / full release of every fan-control variant stays gated behind hardware / bench-evidence / compliance gates. This file remains documentation / source-of-truth metadata only: it adds no product YAML, no firmware config string, no artifact_name, no config/webflash-builds.json row, flips no webflash_build_matrix, publishes no firmware, and does NOT touch the WebFlash repo. Per-variant fields record the real firmware-config status verified against config/preview-release-targets.json, config/preview-fan-triac-build-rows.json, config/manual-firmware-artifacts.json, config/product-catalog.json, and config/webflash-builds.json. Missing full-bundle firmware configs are marked preview-planned-missing-config and are NOT silently exposed; a fan-only config (e.g. Ceiling-POE-FanPWM / Ceiling-POE-FanDAC) is deliberately NOT substituted for a room-bundle variant because it omits the bundle's room-sensing modules. Relay / PWM / 0-10V (DAC) / TRIAC control are NOT interchangeable at runtime. Only Bathroom and Kitchen have fan variants; Corridor / Landing, Living Room, and Bedroom remain sensing / status bundles. Bathroom TRIAC is an ADVANCED / manual-warning preview only; its HW-005 buildability is RESOLVED (TRIAC-PINMAP-CORRECT-001) but the advanced-manual-preview PUBLISH (TRIAC-PUBLISH-ADVANCED-PREVIEW-001) stays gated by PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 + the COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001 experimental-lane preconditions (COMPLIANCE-001 closed by market posture; see docs/decisions/COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001.md) — no artifact is cut and no preview / advanced-preview row is added until the commissioning PR lands, and the acknowledgement gate is an additional UX warning on top of those publish gates, never a substitute. Kitchen has NO TRIAC variant. See docs/sense360-room-bundles.md.

warning_copy.preview: PREVIEW FIRMWARE — buildable and installable for testers only. This build is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and NOT a customer default. No bench evidence and no compliance is claimed. Flash at your own risk and expect to recover with the rescue/stable firmware.
warning_copy.advanced-preview: ADVANCED PREVIEW — MAINS-VOLTAGE RISK. This firmware drives mains-voltage hardware (e.g. TRIAC phase-control dimming) and is for competent persons performing a manual installation only. It is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and is NEVER a default. No bench evidence and no electrical-safety / compliance certification is claimed. Incorrect installation can cause fire, electric shock, or death. Do not install unless you are qualified to work on mains wiring.
warning_copy.preview-planned-missing-config: PLANNED PREVIEW — NO FIRMWARE BUILT YET. The full room-bundle firmware config for this fan-control variant has not been built or published, so it is NOT installable, NOT released, NOT buyable, and NOT exposed in WebFlash. A fan-only firmware config (e.g. Ceiling-POE-FanPWM / Ceiling-POE-FanDAC) is deliberately NOT substituted because it omits the bundle's room-sensing modules. This variant becomes an Advanced-install-only preview only after its full-composition firmware config is built and imported; stable / full release additionally requires hardware, bench evidence, and compliance sign-off.
warning_copy.preview-compile-validated: PREVIEW (COMPILE-VALIDATED, PREVIEW .bin PUBLISHED) — the full room-bundle firmware config EXISTS (product YAML authored), its hosted ESPHome compile has PASSED (Compile-only Firmware Validation run 26913592989; full ESPHome compile success), and an Advanced-install-only preview .bin has now been published to the shared v1.0.0-preview release (Room-Bundle Fan Firmware Publish run 26947595936; firmware-build / release proof only). This build is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, NOT a customer default, and NOT buyable. No bench evidence and no compliance is claimed. It is NOT exposed as a WebFlash one-click import. A fan-only firmware config (e.g. Ceiling-POE-FanPWM / Ceiling-POE-FanDAC) is deliberately NOT substituted because it omits the bundle's room-sensing modules.

fan_variant_candidates.name: Bathroom Bundle, PoE + Relay Fan Control

composition_check.note: Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-FanRelay-RoomIQ carries the Bathroom room modules (VentIQ + RoomIQ) plus the FanRelay driver; it is a correct full-bundle composition, not a fan-only config.

stable_blockers: Mains-safety / installation-approval / creepage / clearance evidence + competent-person sign-off + GPIO3 strap-pin boot characterisation (S360-310).
Shared S360-410 PoE PSU schematic verification (PRODUCT-POE-410-001).
fan_variant_candidates.warning_copy: PREVIEW FIRMWARE — buildable and installable for testers only. This build is NOT hardware verified, NOT stable, NOT recommended, and NOT a customer default. No bench evidence and no compliance is claimed. Flash at your own risk and expect to recover with the rescue/stable firmware.
fan_variant_candidates.notes: Relay on/off extractor control (S360-310) on top of the stable Bathroom PoE bundle. This is the ONLY fan-control room-bundle variant whose full-composition firmware config is built and published today: it is a real PREVIEW target on the manual-preview lane (config/preview-release-targets.json target 'ceiling-poe-ventiq-fanrelay-roomiq-preview'; config/preview-fan-triac-build-rows.json). It is therefore WebFlash-easy-mode preview-eligible as an Advanced-install-only, acknowledgement-gated preview bundle. webflash_exposed stays false because no fan row is added to config/webflash-builds.json; the actual WebFlash one-click exposure is the separately queued downstream WebFlash-repo follow-up (WF-IMPORT-RELAY-001). Not stable, not recommended, not a default, not buyable.

fan_variant_candidates.name: Bathroom Bundle, PoE + TRIAC AC Fan Control (Advanced / manual)

firmware_config_evidence.build_blocker_history: HW-005 BUILDABILITY is RESOLVED (TRIAC-PINMAP-CORRECT-001): the SX1509-free S360-100-R4 Core routes the J15 TRIAC nets TRI_GPIO1/TRI_GPIO2 direct to interrupt-capable ESP32-S3 GPIOs (gate IO14 -> U1 MOC3023M opto-triac; zero-cross IO13 -> OK1 EL814 zero-cross opto-coupler), replacing the placeholder GPIO5/GPIO6 that collided with RoomIQ on J10, so ESPHome ac_dimmer attaches its interrupt/ISR. The full composition compiles (local full ESPHome compile, success); schematic_status is schematic-backed, NOT bench-verified. No buildability blocker remains. The former advanced-manual-preview PUBLISH gate (PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 + the COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001 experimental-lane preconditions) is now EXECUTED by TRIAC-COMMISSIONING-001 (see advanced_preview_publish_gate below).

gate_resolution.PACKAGE-TRIAC-001: satisfied — operator bench protocol complete and signed attestation committed (docs/package-triac-001-operator-bench-proof.md): Steps A-F PASS on the real Manrose fan-motor load, full-composition re-confirm PASS via closure path (a), six-cell operator attestation recorded.
gate_resolution.COMPLIANCE-001: closed by market posture (docs/decisions/COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001.md): the S360-320 is never placed on the market, so the conformity-assessment obligation does not arise; this gate element resolves to that record's experimental-lane preconditions, which are met.

advanced_preview_publish_gate.note: TRIAC-PUBLISH-ADVANCED-PREVIEW-001 was the PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 AND COMPLIANCE-001-element publish gate, NOT an acknowledgement gate. TRIAC-COMMISSIONING-001 (the only PR permitted to re-key it) EXECUTED it: PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 is satisfied (operator-attested bench proof) and COMPLIANCE-001 is closed by market posture, so FanTRIAC moved into the experimental self-build mains lane (EXPERIMENTAL-SELF-BUILD-MAINS-LANE) and gained a config/webflash-builds.json row on the new experimental build channel. The gated_by tokens are preserved for traceability. artifact_cut stays false (this commissioning does NOT cut a release tag) and preview_or_advanced_preview_row_added stays false (the row is on the experimental channel, not the preview / advanced-preview channel); experimental_row_added is true. Despite executing the gate, FanTRIAC stays NEVER stable, NEVER recommended, NEVER a customer default, NEVER buyable, NEVER in any kit or kit picker; the S360-320 is a self-build CERN-OHL-P board Sense360 never places on the market. The advanced (mains-risk) acknowledgement gate remains an additional UX warning on top of this publish gate, never a substitute. Downstream WebFlash one-click import stays the separate WF-IMPORT-TRIAC-001 follow-up.

composition_check.note: The intended config Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-FanTRIAC-RoomIQ is a correct full-bundle composition (VentIQ + RoomIQ + FanTRIAC). HW-005 buildability is RESOLVED (TRIAC-PINMAP-CORRECT-001) and it compiles. TRIAC-COMMISSIONING-001 moved it into the experimental self-build mains lane: it now has a config/webflash-builds.json row on the experimental build channel, so the release matrix can build the .bin on a v1.0.0-experimental tag (no tag is cut by the commissioning itself). It stays NEVER stable / recommended / default / buyable / kit-exposed; the S360-320 is a self-build CERN-OHL-P board Sense360 never places on the market.

stable_blockers: PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 is SATISFIED (operator bench complete + signed attestation committed, docs/package-triac-001-operator-bench-proof.md) and is NO LONGER a blocker; cleared by TRIAC-COMMISSIONING-001. It is listed here only as history of the experimental-lane entry precondition.

STABLE remains gated (experimental-lane commissioning unblocks the experimental publish only, never stable): a stable customer release would be a placing-on-the-market act, which reopens COMPLIANCE-001 and requires an external safety + EMC assessment BEFORE that act (docs/decisions/COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001.md section 4). The S360-320 is a self-build CERN-OHL-P board Sense360 never places on the market.
Shared S360-410 PoE PSU schematic verification (PRODUCT-POE-410-001).
HW-005 buildability is RESOLVED (TRIAC-PINMAP-CORRECT-001) and is no longer a blocker; it is listed here only as history.

fan_variant_candidates.notes: TRIAC AC (mains phase-cut) fan control (S360-320) for the Bathroom bundle. Offered ONLY via the experimental self-build mains lane — never a normal recommended / easy / default option. TRIAC-COMMISSIONING-001 EXECUTED the advanced-manual-preview PUBLISH gate (TRIAC-PUBLISH-ADVANCED-PREVIEW-001): PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 is satisfied (operator-attested bench proof docs/package-triac-001-operator-bench-proof.md) and COMPLIANCE-001 is closed by market posture (docs/decisions/COMPLIANCE-001-RESOLUTION-001.md), so FanTRIAC moved into the experimental lane and gained a config/webflash-builds.json row on the new experimental build channel (artifact Sense360-Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-FanTRIAC-RoomIQ-v1.0.0-experimental.bin). This commissioning does NOT cut a release tag; it is release-eligibility metadata only. It is NOT WebFlash-easy-mode eligible and stays out of every kit / kit picker; downstream WebFlash one-click import is the separate WF-IMPORT-TRIAC-001 follow-up. NEVER stable, NEVER recommended, NEVER a customer default, NEVER buyable. Stable additionally remains gated by the COMPLIANCE-001 reopen trigger (a future market-placement act requires external safety + EMC assessment first) and the shared S360-410 PoE PSU verification (PRODUCT-POE-410-001). The bench proof is firmware-build + functional bench evidence (operator-attested) only — NO electrical-safety / EMC / compliance proof is claimed. The advanced (mains-risk) acknowledgement gate is an additional UX warning on top of the publish gate, never a substitute. Kitchen has no TRIAC variant by policy.

fan_variant_candidates.name: Bathroom Bundle, PoE + PWM (low-voltage / DC) Fan Control

publish_evidence.release_tag_note: Published to the shared v1.0.0-preview preview release — the intended single preview release for every preview artifact (room-bundle + LED + single-driver fan + room-bundle fan) under RELEASE-PREVIEW-FAN-SHARED-TAG-001. softprops/action-gh-release upserted the release, refreshing its name / body / checksums / build-info manifest (a release-metadata refresh, not a release error); the pre-existing room-bundle, LED, and single-driver fan preview .bin stay attached with their SHA256 intact. WebFlash import eligibility is controlled separately by WebFlash import policy and is NOT implied by presence in this shared release. See docs/room-bundle-fan-publish-results.md.

firmware_config_evidence.note: ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-CONFIGS-001 authored the full-composition product YAML (shim over the bundle) and registered the compile-only target; ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-COMPILE-RESULTS-001 then recorded compile_validation_status validated-full-compile from the hosted Compile-only Firmware Validation workflow (run 26913592989, ref main, compile_mode=full, 2026-06-04, ESPHome 2026.4.5; Metadata Validation success, Full ESPHome Compile success); no compile fabricated. Firmware-build proof only: still NOT published, no .bin, no WebFlash build row, no preview release target — compile validation is not publication or exposure.

composition_check.note: Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-FanPWM-RoomIQ carries the base room modules (VentIQ + RoomIQ) PLUS the FanPWM driver; it is a correct full-bundle composition, not a fan-only config substitution.

webflash_import_eligibility.webflash_build_row_note: HW-RELEASE-001 (docs/hw-release-001.md, owner decision of record, 2026-07-09) retired the bench-proof documentation gate and added the config/webflash-builds.json release-eligibility metadata row for this config (release_state metadata-ready-unpublished; no binary / Release / tag / manifest cut). Channel teeth: FanPWM / FanDAC rows sit on the preview channel, FanRelay rows on the experimental channel ONLY (COMPLIANCE-001 mains-adjacent lane posture) — NEVER stable. Stable stays blocked with its blockers unchanged, one-click WebFlash exposure stays gated, and FANDAC-I2C-ADDR-001 bench verification stays pending.
webflash_import_eligibility.reason: Preview / manual-preview WebFlash import is ELIGIBLE (ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-WEBFLASH-ELIGIBILITY-001): the published v1.0.0-preview artifact may be imported into WebFlash as an Advanced-install-only, acknowledgement-gated PREVIEW bundle build. Eligibility authorises a preview import only -- it does NOT make the config stable, recommended, a customer default, or buyable; it adds NO config/webflash-builds.json row (the fan-token guardrail and the catalog webflash_build_matrix=false both stand); and it grants NO Simple / easy-mode exposure, which stays gated behind the downstream WebFlash import plus acknowledgement / warning UX (WF-IMPORT-FAN-BUNDLES-001). Missing hardware / bench / compliance evidence blocks stable / full release only, never this preview import. Firmware-build / release proof only.

publish_evidence.release_tag_note: Published to the shared v1.0.0-preview preview release — the intended single preview release for every preview artifact (room-bundle + LED + single-driver fan + room-bundle fan) under RELEASE-PREVIEW-FAN-SHARED-TAG-001. softprops/action-gh-release upserted the release, refreshing its name / body / checksums / build-info manifest (a release-metadata refresh, not a release error); the pre-existing room-bundle, LED, and single-driver fan preview .bin stay attached with their SHA256 intact. WebFlash import eligibility is controlled separately by WebFlash import policy and is NOT implied by presence in this shared release. See docs/room-bundle-fan-publish-results.md.
publish_evidence.fandac_address_note: Built WITH the FanDAC GP8403 IC2 address override fan_dac_2_i2c_address: "0x5A" (IC1 0x58; 0x59 forbidden — it collides with the air-quality SGP41 at 0x59). This is a compile-time / firmware configuration only; the physical S360-312 IC2 DIP-switch (SW2) -> I2C address mapping is NOT bench verified and stays pending under FANDAC-I2C-ADDR-001.

firmware_config_evidence.note: ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-CONFIGS-001 authored the full-composition product YAML (shim over the bundle) and registered the compile-only target; ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-COMPILE-RESULTS-001 then recorded compile_validation_status validated-full-compile from the hosted Compile-only Firmware Validation workflow (run 26913592989, ref main, compile_mode=full, 2026-06-04, ESPHome 2026.4.5; Metadata Validation success, Full ESPHome Compile success); no compile fabricated. Firmware-build proof only: still NOT published, no .bin, no WebFlash build row, no preview release target — compile validation is not publication or exposure.

fan_dac_address_requirement.required_hardware_switch: S360-312 IC2 DIP switch (SW2) set so IC2 = 0x5A

composition_check.note: Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-FanDAC-RoomIQ carries the base room modules (VentIQ + RoomIQ) PLUS the FanDAC driver; it is a correct full-bundle composition, not a fan-only config substitution. The FanDAC second GP8403 (IC2) is relocated to 0x5A so it does not collide with the air-quality SGP41 @ 0x59.

webflash_import_eligibility.fandac_note: Import eligibility does NOT bench-verify the S360-312 IC2 DIP-switch -> I2C address mapping. The preview .bin was built with the fan_dac_2_i2c_address "0x5A" override only (IC1 0x58; 0x59 forbidden -- it collides with the air-quality SGP41 at 0x59), and FANDAC-I2C-ADDR-001 stays pending. No FanDAC hardware verification is claimed.

stable_blockers: Full-composition firmware config authored and now compile-validated (compile-only target registered, compile_validation_status validated-full-compile via hosted Compile-only Firmware Validation run 26913592989) but still NOT published, NOT WebFlash-exposed, and NOT hardware verified — stable additionally requires hardware, bench evidence, and compliance sign-off.
FANDAC-I2C-ADDR-001: bench-verify the S360-312 DIP-position -> I2C address mapping (IC1=0x58, IC2=0x5A) on the shared core_i2c bus with the air-quality module (SGP41 @ 0x59) present.
Cloudlift S12 / J3 harness + product-bench evidence; S360-312 schematic / BOM.
Shared S360-410 PoE PSU schematic verification (PRODUCT-POE-410-001).

WebFlash import eligibility is controlled separately by WebFlash import policy and is NOT implied by presence in this shared release. See docs/room-bundle-fan-publish-results.md.
publish_evidence.fandac_address_note: Built WITH the FanDAC GP8403 IC2 address override fan_dac_2_i2c_address: "0x5A" (IC1 0x58; 0x59 forbidden — it collides with the air-quality SGP41 at 0x59). This is a compile-time / firmware configuration only; the physical S360-312 IC2 DIP-switch (SW2) -> I2C address mapping is NOT bench verified and stays pending under FANDAC-I2C-ADDR-001.

firmware_config_evidence.note: ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-CONFIGS-001 authored the full-composition product YAML (shim over the bundle) and registered the compile-only target; ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-COMPILE-RESULTS-001 then recorded compile_validation_status validated-full-compile from the hosted Compile-only Firmware Validation workflow (run 26913592989, ref main, compile_mode=full, 2026-06-04, ESPHome 2026.4.5; Metadata Validation success, Full ESPHome Compile success); no compile fabricated. composition_check.note: Ceiling-POE-AirIQ-FanDAC-RoomIQ carries the base room modules (AirIQ + RoomIQ) PLUS the FanDAC driver; it is a correct full-bundle composition, not a fan-only config substitution. webflash_import_eligibility.webflash_build_row_note: HW-RELEASE-001 (docs/hw-release-001.md, owner decision of record, 2026-07-09) retired the bench-proof documentation gate and added the config/webflash-builds.json release-eligibility metadata row for this config (release_state metadata-ready-unpublished; no binary / Release / tag / manifest cut). Channel teeth: FanPWM / FanDAC rows sit on the preview channel, FanRelay rows on the experimental channel ONLY (COMPLIANCE-001 mains-adjacent lane posture) — NEVER stable. webflash_import_eligibility.reason: Preview / manual-preview WebFlash import is ELIGIBLE (ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-WEBFLASH-ELIGIBILITY-001): the published v1.0.0-preview artifact may be imported into WebFlash as an Advanced-install-only, acknowledgement-gated PREVIEW bundle build. Eligibility authorises a preview import only -- it does NOT make the config stable, recommended, a customer default, or buyable; it adds NO config/webflash-builds.json row (the fan-token guardrail and the catalog webflash_build_matrix=false both stand); and it grants NO Simple / easy-mode exposure, which stays gated behind the downstream WebFlash import plus acknowledgement / warning UX (WF-IMPORT-FAN-BUNDLES-001). Missing hardware / bench / compliance evidence blocks stable / full release only, never this preview import. Firmware-build / release proof only.
webflash_import_eligibility.fandac_note: Import eligibility does NOT bench-verify the S360-312 IC2 DIP-switch -> I2C address mapping. No FanDAC hardware verification is claimed.

revision_note: ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-CONFIGS-001 built the missing full-composition product YAMLs for the five buildable fan-control variants (Bathroom PWM/DAC, Kitchen Relay/DAC/PWM): each has a real products/ shim + products/bundles/ composition + a config/product-catalog.json hardware-pending row + a config/compile-only-targets.json target. ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-COMPILE-RESULTS-001 then recorded the hosted full ESPHome compile result for those five configs and promoted them from buildable-preview-compile-pending to buildable-preview-compile-validated: the Compile-only Firmware Validation workflow (run 26913592989, ref main, compile_mode=full, 2026-06-04, ESPHome 2026.4.5) passed Metadata Validation and the Full ESPHome Compile, so each variant's firmware_config_evidence.compile_validation_status is now validated-full-compile with a compile_evidence block. The two FanDAC configs compiled with the REQUIRED 0x5A IC2 address override (see fan_dac_i2c_address_policy); the physical DIP-switch -> address mapping stays bench-pending under FANDAC-I2C-ADDR-001. This is firmware-build proof only: TRIAC's HW-005 buildability is RESOLVED (TRIAC-PINMAP-CORRECT-001) but its advanced-manual-preview publish stays gated by PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 + COMPLIANCE-001; Bathroom Relay is unchanged. No firmware published, no .bin, no release/tag, no WebFlash repo change, no config/webflash-builds.json row, nothing stable / recommended / default / buyable, no hardware / bench / compliance / safety proof.

GP8403 0x59 must not be used with VentIQ/AirIQ (SGP41 @ 0x59 collision).

SUPERSEDED BY HW-RELEASE-001 (docs/hw-release-001.md, 2026-07-09): the former fan-token guardrail was revised by the owner into channel teeth (fan configs never on the stable channel) and the five room-bundle fan configs now carry release-eligibility metadata rows (FanPWM/FanDAC preview; FanRelay experimental only; release_state metadata-ready-unpublished). One-click WebFlash exposure stays the separately gated downstream follow-up.

webflash_import_eligibility_decision.rule: The five published full-composition Bathroom / Kitchen fan-control preview bundle artifacts (Bathroom PWM / DAC, Kitchen Relay / DAC / PWM) are now preview WebFlash-import ELIGIBLE: each variant carries webflash_import_eligibility.eligible=true with exposure_class=acknowledgement-gated and channel=preview, so downstream WebFlash no longer treats the catalog webflash_build_matrix=false as a preview-import blocker. Eligibility authorises an Advanced-install-only preview import only. It does NOT promote anything to stable / full release, adds NO config/webflash-builds.json row (the fan-token guardrail and catalog webflash_build_matrix=false both stand), and grants NO Simple / easy-mode exposure -- that stays gated behind the downstream WebFlash import plus the fan-preview acknowledgement / warning UX (WF-IMPORT-FAN-BUNDLES-001). FanTRIAC (Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-FanTRIAC-RoomIQ) stays excluded (not WebFlash-import eligible, no artifact; HW-005 buildability RESOLVED by TRIAC-PINMAP-CORRECT-001 but the advanced-manual-preview PUBLISH gated by PACKAGE-TRIAC-001 + COMPLIANCE-001) and is handled by its own separate TRIAC-specific track. The stable Bathroom PoE build (Ceiling-POE-VentIQ-RoomIQ) and Simple install are unchanged.
webflash_import_eligibility_decision.hw_release_001_posture_note: The guarantees above are the 2026-06-04 ROOM-BUNDLE-FAN-WEBFLASH-ELIGIBILITY-001 decision's OWN scope (that decision added no build row and flipped no catalog field) and remain historically accurate. HW-RELEASE-001 (docs/hw-release-001.md, owner decision of record, 2026-07-09) later retired the bench-proof documentation gate and, by its own separate declaration, added config/webflash-builds.json release-eligibility metadata rows for the five configs (FanPWM/FanDAC preview; FanRelay experimental only; never stable) and flipped their config/product-catalog.json entries to status preview / webflash_build_matrix true. Unchanged: stable stays blocked with all blockers intact, nothing becomes recommended / default / buyable / kit-exposed, Simple easy-mode stays gated behind the downstream WebFlash import + warning gates, and FANDAC-I2C-ADDR-001 bench verification stays pending.

fan_dac_i2c_address_policy.rationale: The S360-312 FanDAC board carries two GP8403 DACs (package defaults IC1 @ 0x58, IC2 @ 0x59). Both Sense360 air-quality modules — VentIQ (S360-211) and AirIQ (S360-210) — carry an SGP41 VOC/NOx sensor at I2C 0x59 on the shared core_i2c bus. So the FanDAC default IC2 @ 0x59 collides with the air-quality SGP41 in any VentIQ/AirIQ + FanDAC bundle.
fan_dac_i2c_address_policy.precise_rule: AirIQ/VentIQ conflict with FanDAC ONLY when the FanDAC GP8403 uses 0x59. With GP8403 IC2 relocated to a non-0x59 address (0x5A), VentIQ/AirIQ + FanDAC can coexist on the shared bus. GP8403 supports the address span 0x58-0x5F via its A0/A1/A2 hardware address pins.
fan_dac_i2c_address_policy.firmware_override: fan_dac_2_i2c_address: "0x5A" (set in the room-bundle product YAML; overrides the package default 0x59)
fan_dac_i2c_address_policy.required_hardware_switch: The installer MUST set the S360-312 IC2 DIP switch (SW2) so IC2 reports 0x5A (A1=1; A0=A2=0), NOT the 0x59 default. IC1 (SW1) stays at 0x58.
fan_dac_i2c_address_policy.webflash_one_click_posture: The fandac_conflicts_with_airiq mutex in config/webflash-compatibility.json STAYS in force for the WebFlash one-click surface, which cannot set DIP switches; AirIQ + FanDAC stays out of the WebFlash firmware-combination grammar. These room-bundle DAC configs are advanced / manual previews behind the documented switch requirement, NOT one-click WebFlash builds. The conflict is NOT relaxed silently — it is refined to a documented hardware-address requirement.
